content.problemStatement:
Stack likes the number 333 a lot.

He has two non-negative integers AAA and BBB.

In one operation, Stack can do either of the following:


A:=∣A−B∣A:=|A-B|A:=∣A−B∣ (change AAA to ∣A−B∣|A-B|∣A−B∣)
B:=∣A−B∣B:=|A-B|B:=∣A−B∣ (change BBB to ∣A−B∣|A-B|∣A−B∣)
Note that ∣X∣|X|∣X∣ denotes absolute value of XXX. For example ∣−7∣=7|-7| = 7∣−7∣=7 and ∣4∣=4|4| = 4∣4∣=4.

Find the **minimum** number of operations after which **at least** one integer out of AAA and BBB becomes divisible by 333.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The only line of each test case contains two integers AAA and BBB.


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line the **minimum** number of operations after which **at least** one integer out of AAA and BBB becomes divisible by 333.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: A=0A=0A=0 is already divisible by 333.
Test case 222: In the only operation, Stack can change A=1A=1A=1 to A=∣A−B∣=∣1−1∣=0A = |A-B| = |1-1| = 0A=∣A−B∣=∣1−1∣=0. Now A=0A=0A=0 is divisible by 333.
